attacking_style: Opportunistic rather than premeditated attacker. Shows unexpected punch on steep, short climbs despite being built for sustained efforts, capitalizing on moments when others hesitate.
team_tactics: Limited observable team support in data. Appears positioned as protected rider but relies on individual positioning instincts rather than coordinated leadouts.
terrain_preference: Best suited to sustained gradients where GC engine excels, but demonstrated surprising adaptability to explosive finishes like Mur de Huy. Underestimated on steep, short power climbs.
vulnerability: Insufficient data to identify clear tactical vulnerabilities, though typical GC rider profile suggests potential weakness in pure sprint situations or early-race aggression.
key_patterns: Opportunistic positioning: actively follows sprints and attacks rather than passive riding (2 instances)
Exceeds expectations on explosive climbs despite sustained-effort physiology (Mur de Huy podium)
Shows tactical awareness to maximize placement through active engagement in finales (2 instances)
form_trajectory: positive: Strong spring 2022 form with Fleche Wallonne podium and active GC racing at Romandie showing sharpness across terrain types
